title: Statistical Inference, Bayesian Updating, and Prediction · Model Repair Bay
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving evidence-based prediction. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reproducible advanced statistics computational study and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate likelihood estimation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive interval test theory in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute bayesian models in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: The observed values are 11, 15, 19, 15 minutes. Calculate their mean. Use the result to decide Predictive Validation for a reproducible advanced statistics computational study.
steps.why: The Predictive Validation result for a reproducible advanced statistics computational study is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
